```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
mu0  <- sample(c(4, 6, 8), 1)
n    <- sample(c(25, 36, 49, 64), 1)
xbar <- round(runif(1, mu0 - 1, mu0 + 2), 2)
s2   <- round(runif(1, 1, 9), 2)

## Estatistica t e TRV
t_stat <- round(sqrt(n) * (xbar - mu0) / sqrt(s2), 4)
lrts   <- round(n * log(1 + t_stat^2 / (n - 1)), 4)

## Respostas
correct <- lrts
w1 <- round(t_stat^2, 4)
w2 <- round(n * log(1 + t_stat^2 / n), 4)
w3 <- round(2 * n * log(1 + t_stat / (n - 1)), 4)
w4 <- round(n * (1 + t_stat^2 / (n - 1)) - n, 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} N(\mu,\sigma^2)$ com $\mu$ e $\sigma^2$ ambos desconhecidos, testando $H_0:\mu = `r mu0`$. O TRV simplifica para $-2\log\Lambda = n\log(1 + T^2/(n-1))$, onde $T = \sqrt{n}(\bar{X}-\mu_0)/S$. Com $\bar{X} = `r xbar`$, $S^2 = `r s2`$, $n = `r n`$, temos $T = `r t_stat`$. O valor de $-2\log\Lambda$ e:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
$-2\log\Lambda = `r n` \times \log\!\left(1 + \frac{`r t_stat`^2}{`r n-1`}\right) = `r n` \times \log(`r round(1 + t_stat^2/(n-1),4)`) = `r lrts`$.

Note que $T^2$ e a estatistica $F$ de Snedecor com 1 e $n-1$ graus de liberdade.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap6S5Q6_TRV_Normal_sigma_desconhecido
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
